channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Can LLM Agents Be CFOs? A Benchmark for Resource Allocation in Dynamic Enterprise Environments
item.description: arXiv:2603.23638v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have enabled agentic systems that can reason, plan, and act across complex tasks, but it remains unclear whether they can allocate resources effectively under uncertainty. Unlike short-horizon reactive decisions, allocatio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, LLM, tool, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GTO Wizard Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2603.23660v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce GTO Wizard Benchmark, a public API and standardized evaluation framework for benchmarking algorithms in Heads-Up No-Limit Texas Hold'em (HUNL). The benchmark evaluates agents against GTO Wizard AI, a state-of-the-art superhuman poker agen...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> API, reasoning, ICL, LLM, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning-guided Prioritized Planning for Lifelong Multi-Agent Path Finding in Warehouse Automation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Lifelong Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) is critical for modern warehouse automation, which requires multiple robots to continuously navigate conflict-free paths to optimize the overall system throughput. However, the complexity of warehouse environme...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, framework, arxiv, RAG, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: VehicleMemBench: An Executable Benchmark for Multi-User Long-Term Memory in In-Vehicle Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.23840v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: With the growing demand for intelligent in-vehicle experiences, vehicle-based agents are evolving from simple assistants to long-term companions. This evolution requires agents to continuously model multi-user preferences and make reliable decisions i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, ICL, LLM, release, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Implicit Turn-Wise Policy Optimization for Proactive User-LLM Interaction
item.description: arXiv:2603.23550v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-turn human-AI collaboration is fundamental to deploying interactive services such as adaptive tutoring, conversational recommendation, and professional consultation. However, optimizing these interactions via reinforcement learning is hindered b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, LLM, analysis, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Safe Reinforcement Learning with Preference-based Constraint Inference
item.description: arXiv:2603.23565v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safe reinforcement learning (RL) is a standard paradigm for safety-critical decision making. However, real-world safety constraints can be complex, subjective, and even hard to explicitly specify. Existing works on constraint inference rely on restric...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> demonstration, study, model, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Causal Reconstruction of Sentiment Signals from Sparse News Data
item.description: arXiv:2603.23568v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sentiment signals derived from sparse news are commonly used in financial analysis and technology monitoring, yet transforming raw article-level observations into reliable temporal series remains a largely unsolved engineering problem. Rather than tre...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> ICL, framework, analysis, arxiv, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Masks: Efficient, Flexible Diffusion Language Models via Deletion-Insertion Processes
item.description: arXiv:2603.23507v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While Masked Diffusion Language Models (MDLMs) relying on token masking and unmasking have shown promise in language modeling, their computational efficiency and generation flexibility remain constrained by the masking paradigm. In this paper, we prop...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, paper, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Grounding Vision and Language to 3D Masks for Long-Horizon Box Rearrangement
item.description: arXiv:2603.23676v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study long-horizon planning in 3D environments from under-specified natural-language goals using only visual observations, focusing on multi-step 3D box rearrangement tasks. Existing approaches typically rely on symbolic planners with brittle relat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, reasoning, vision, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Upper Entropy for 2-Monotone Lower Probabilities
item.description: arXiv:2603.23558v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Uncertainty quantification is a key aspect in many tasks such as model selection/regularization, or quantifying prediction uncertainties to perform active learning or OOD detection. Within credal approaches that consider modeling uncertainty as probab...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, analysis, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Accuracy: Introducing a Symbolic-Mechanistic Approach to Interpretable Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.23517v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accuracy-based evaluation cannot reliably distinguish genuine generalization from shortcuts like memorization, leakage, or brittle heuristics, especially in small-data regimes. In this position paper, we argue for mechanism-aware evaluation that combi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PoiCGAN: A Targeted Poisoning Based on Feature-Label Joint Perturbation in Federated Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.23574v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Federated Learning (FL), as a popular distributed learning paradigm, has shown outstanding performance in improving computational efficiency and protecting data privacy, and is widely applied in industrial image classification. However, due to its dis...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, model, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
